InterServer Management API

Overview

The InterServer Management API provides programmatic access to manage your InterServer services. Use this REST API to automate provisioning, configuration, and billing operations across your account.

The API covers the following service categories:

  • Domains — registration, transfers, and DNS management
  • Web Hosting — shared and reseller hosting
  • VPS Hosting — virtual private servers
  • Dedicated Servers — bare metal and Rapid Deploy Servers
  • Backups — storage and backup services
  • Licenses — control panel and software licenses
  • Mail — mail delivery services
  • SSL — certificate provisioning
  • Billing — invoices, payment methods, and account management

For interactive testing, see the API documentation.

Authentication

Most endpoints require authentication. Two methods are supported:

API Key (Preferred)

Generate an API key from the Account Security page on my.interserver.net. Pass it in the X-API-KEY request header:

X-API-KEY: your-api-key-here

Session-Based Authentication

Alternatively, authenticate by creating a session:

  1. Log in — Send a POST request to /login with your account credentials. The response includes a session identifier.
  2. Pass the session ID — Include the session identifier in the sessionid header on subsequent requests:
sessionid: your-session-id-here

API key authentication is recommended for most integrations as it does not expire and avoids the overhead of session management.

More information: https://www.interserver.net/contact-us.html
Contact Info: support@interserver.net
Version: 0.9.0
BasePath:/apiv2
MIT License
https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT

Access

  1. APIKey KeyParamName:X-API-KEY KeyInQuery:false KeyInHeader:true
  2. APIKey KeyParamName:sessionid KeyInQuery:false KeyInHeader:true
  3. APIKey KeyParamName:sessionid KeyInQuery:false KeyInHeader:false

Account

Up 
post /account/username
Change Account Username (changeAccountUsername)
Changes the login username for the account. The new username must be unique across all accounts. After changing, use the new username for all future logins.

Return type

TextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "text" : "You were successfull."
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Response with a text message field. TextResponse

400

The specified resource was not found getAccountInfo_401_response

Example data

Content-Type:

Example data

Content-Type:

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
delete /account/oauth/{name}
Unlink OAuth Account (deleteAccountOauthName)
Unlinks a third-party OAuth provider from the account. After unlinking, that provider can no longer be used for login.

Path parameters

name (required)
Path Parameter — default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
delete /account/2fa
Disable Two-Factor Authentication (deleteAccountTfa)
Disables two-factor authentication on the account. After disabling, the account will only require password-based authentication.

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
patch /account/iplimits
Remove IP Access Restriction (deleteIpLimit)
Removes an IP address range from the account's access restriction list. If this is the last range, IP limiting is effectively disabled and the account becomes accessible from any IP address.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json

Return type

GenericResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "status" : "ok",
  "text" : "The command completed successfully."
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

IP Range removed. GenericResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /account
Retrieve Account Details (getAccountInfo)
Returns the full account profile including contact information, billing address, and security settings. Use this to populate account management forms or verify account state before making changes with POST /account.

Return type

AccountInfo

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "custid" : "98651",
  "ima" : "client",
  "data" : {
    "group" : "0",
    "address" : "112 Uber St.",

get /account/2fa
Get Two-Factor Setup Data (getAccountTfaSetup)
Returns the TOTP secret key needed to configure a two-factor authentication app (e.g. Google Authenticator). Present the 2fa_google_key as a QR code or display the 2fa_google_split value for manual entry. After setup, verify with POST /account/2fa.

Return type

getAccountTfaSetup_200_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "2fa_google_key" : "2fa_google_key",
  "2fa_google_split" : "2fa_google_split"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Two-factor authentication setup data. getAccountTfaSetup_200_response

get /home

Returns an overview of the account suitable for a dashboard home page, including service counts, recent activity, and account-level alerts. Requires an authenticated session.

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /logout
Log Out (logout)
Invalidates the current session. After calling this endpoint, the session token can no longer be used for authenticated requests. The client should discard the stored session ID.

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

post /account/apikey
Generate New API Key (updateAccountApiKey)
Generates a new API key for the account. The previous key is immediately invalidated. Store the new key securely as it cannot be retrieved later.

SuccessTextResponse

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
post /account/features
Update Account Feature Flags (updateAccountFeatures)
Updates account-level feature flags that control service capabilities. These flags can disable password reset, OS reinstall, or other potentially destructive operations across your services. Changes take effect immediately.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • multipart/form-data
  • application/json

Form parameters

disable_reset (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null format: int32
disable_reinstall (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null format: int32

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "text" : "You were successfull."
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

422

Request was valid but did not contain updatable values. TextResponse

Example data

Content-Type:
Up 
post /account
Update Account Information (updateAccountInfo)
Updates the stored contact and billing information on your account. Submit only the fields you want to change. Validation errors are returned as a 422 response with field-level messages.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • multipart/form-data
  • application/json

Form parameters

name (required)
Form Parameter — Your name. default: null
company (optional)
Form Parameter — Your company name. default: null
address (required)
Form Parameter — Your address. default: null
address2 (optional)
Form Parameter — Additional address information. default: null
city (required)
Form Parameter — Your city. default: null
state (required)
Form Parameter — Your state. default: null
zip (required)
Form Parameter — Your ZIP code. default: null
country (required)
Form Parameter — Your country. default: null
phone (required)
Form Parameter — Your phone number. default: null
locale (optional)
Form Parameter — Your preferred locale. default: null
email_invoices (optional)
Form Parameter — Your email for invoice notifications. default: null
email_abuse (optional)
Form Parameter — Your email for abuse notifications. default: null
disable_reset (optional)
Form Parameter — Set to true to disable account resets, or false to enable them. default: null
disable_reinstall (optional)
Form Parameter — Set to true to disable server reinstalls, or false to enable them. default: null
disable_server_notifications (optional)
Form Parameter — Set to true to disable server notifications, or false to enable them. default: null
disable_email_notifications (optional)
Form Parameter — Set to true to disable email notifications, or false to enable them. default: null
gstin (optional)
Form Parameter — Your GST identification number (if applicable). default: null

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "text" : "You were successfull."
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

422

Validation error while updating account data. TextResponse

Example data

Content-Type:

Example data

Content-Type:

default

Default response
Up 
post /account/iplimits
Add IP Access Restriction (updateAccountIpLimits)
Adds an IP address range to the account's access restriction list. Once IP limiting is active, only requests originating from allowed ranges can access the account. Provide the start and end of the range in dotted-quad notation.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • multipart/form-data
  • application/json

Form parameters

start (required)
Form Parameter — The begining (or first) IP address in the range. default: null
end (required)
Form Parameter — The ending (or last) IP address in the range. default: null

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "text" : "You were successfull."
}

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

422

IP limit payload contains an invalid address. TextResponse

Example data

Content-Type:

default

Default response
Up 
post /account/password

Changes the account login password. The current password must be provided for verification. After a successful change, existing API keys remain valid but active sessions may require re-authentication.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • multipart/form-data
  • application/json

password (required)
Form Parameter — default: null

Backups

post /backups/order
Place Backup Order (addBackup)
Places a new backup storage order and generates an invoice. On success, the response includes invoice IDs that can be used with /billing/invoices/{id} to view the invoice or /pay/{method}/{invoices} to complete payment. The service is provisioned after payment is confirmed.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • multipart/form-data
  • application/json

Form parameters

validateOnly (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
serviceType (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null format: int32
coupon (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null

Return type

BackupOrderPostResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "continue" : true,
  "errors" : [ ],
  "total_cost" : "3.00",
  "iid" : "25296597",
  "iids" : [ "SERVICEbackups40464" ],
  "real_iids" : [ "25296597" ],
  "serviceId" : 40464,
  "invoice_description" : "Storage ST 100",
  "cj_params" : {
    "containerTagId" : "1684",
    "CID" : "1525038",
    "OID" : "backups40464",
    "TYPE" : "355669",
    "ITEM1" : "backups10831",
    "AMT1" : "3.00",
    "QTY1" : 1,
    "CURRENCY" : "USD"
  }
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Response from the backup order call including invoice IDs for payment. BackupOrderPostResponse

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

default

Default response
Up 
delete /backups/{id}
Cancel Backup Service (cancelBackup)
Cancels the specified backup storage service. The service remains accessible until the end of the current billing period. This action cannot be undone; a new order must be placed to restore service.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The backup service ID. Use the backup_id from GET /backups to identify the service. default: null

Return type

cancelBackup_200_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Backups is canceled."
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Backups Cancel cancelBackup_200_response

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /backups/{id}
Get Backup Service Details (getBackupInfo)
Returns detailed service information for the specified backup storage order, including backup_username, backup_ip, backup_status, and backup_quota in serviceInfo. Also returns client_links, billingDetails, extraInfoTables, package, and custCurrency.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The backup service ID. Use the backup_id from GET /backups to identify the service. default: null

Return type

Backup

{

Up 
get /backups/{id}/invoices
Get Backup Order Invoices (getBackupInvoices)
Retrieves invoices associated with the backup storage order. Use this to confirm billing status or locate invoices for payment.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The backup service ID. Use the backup_id from GET /backups to identify the service. default: null

Return type

ChargeInvoiceRows

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "invoices" : {
    "1234565" : {
      "invoices_id" : 1234565,
      "invoices_description" : "Current IP + Scrub",
      "invoices_amount" : 5,
      "invoices_date" : "2025-12-05T19:38:24.000Z",
      "invoices_paid" : 1,
      "invoices_due_date" : "2025-12-19T19:38:24.000Z",
      "invoices_currency" : "USD",
      "currency_symbol" : "$",
      "invoices_date_formatted" : "5th Dec 2025",
      "paid_invoices" : {
        "654321" : {
          "invoices_id" : "654321,",
          "invoices_description" : "Credit Card Payment 2189347824",
          "invoices_amount" : 5,
          "invoices_date" : "2025-12-05T19:38:38.000Z",
          "invoices_currency" : "USD",
          "currency_symbol" : "$",
          "invoices_date_formatted" : "5th Dec 2025",
          "payment_type" : "Credit Card Payment",
          "refund_invoices" : {
            "98765" : {
              "invoices_id" : 98765,
              "invoices_description" : "REFUND: Credit Card Payment 2189347824",
              "invoices_amount" : 5,
              "invoices_date" : "2025-12-16T13:32:22.000Z",
              "invoices_currency" : "USD",
              "currency_symbol" : "$",
              "invoices_date_formatted" : "16th Dec 2025"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Get Invoices response ChargeInvoiceRows

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /backups/{id}/login
Get Backup Storage Panel Login (getBackupLogin)
Creates and returns a login session URL for the backup storage panel. The returned session URL can be used to redirect the user directly into the storage management interface without requiring separate credentials.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The backup service ID. Use the backup_id from GET /backups to identify the service. default: null

Return type

BackupLoginResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "text"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Login session details for the backup storage service. BackupLoginResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /backups
List Backup Services (getBackupsList)
Returns all backup storage services on your account. Each entry includes the backup_id, backup_username, backup_ip, backup_status, and backup_quota. Use the backup_id with /backups/{id} to retrieve full service details or /backups/{id}/login to obtain a storage panel session.

Return type

array[BackupRow]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
[ {
  "backup_id" : "2414",
  "backup_name" : "storage-nj.interserver.net",
  "backup_cost" : "5.99",
  "backup_username" : "detainin2414",
  "backup_status" : "pending-setup",
  "services_name" : "Swift Storage"
}, {
  "backup_id" : "2414",
  "backup_name" : "storage-nj.interserver.net",
  "backup_cost" : "5.99",
  "backup_username" : "detainin2414",
  "backup_status" : "pending-setup",
  "services_name" : "Swift Storage"
} ]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

The listing of backup storage services on your account.

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /backups/{id}/welcome_email
Resend Backup Welcome Email (getBackupsWelcomeEmail)
Resends the welcome email for the specified backup service. The email contains connection credentials and setup instructions. Use this when the original welcome email was lost or never received.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The backup service ID. Use the backup_id from GET /backups to identify the service. default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /backups/order
Get Backup Order Form Data (getNewBackup)
Returns available backup storage plans, pricing tiers, and form metadata needed to build an order form. Use the service type IDs from this response when submitting a validation request via PUT /backups/order or placing an order via POST /backups/order.

Return type

BackupsOrder

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "packageCosts" : {
    "11006" : 84
  },
  "serviceTypes" : {
    "11006" : {
      "services_id" : "11006",
      "services_category" : "702",
      "services_field1" : "",
      "services_field2" : "bandwidth=40000000,quota=40000000,inode=100,uinode=ON,vdomains=2,nsubdomains=5,nemails=0,nemailf=0,nemailml=0,nemailr=0,mysql=5,domainptr=5,ftp=100,aftp=OFF,cgi=ON,php=ON,spam=OFF,cron=OFF,catchall=OFF,ssl=ON,ssh=ON,sysinfo=ON,login_keys=ON,dnscontrol=OFF,suspend_at_limit=ON",
      "services_name" : "Storage ST 700",
      "services_type" : "703",
      "services_buyable" : "1",
      "services_cost" : "84.00",
      "services_module" : "backups"
    }
  }
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Information needed to generate an order form. BackupsOrder

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
post /backups/{id}
Update Backup Information (updateBackupInfo)
Updates backup storage service metadata, such as stored credentials or settings for the order.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The backup service ID. Use the backup_id from GET /backups to identify the service. default: null

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

default

Default response
Up 
put /backups/order
Validate Backup Order (validateBackupOrder)
Validates a backup storage order without placing it, returning calculated pricing and any validation errors. Use this to display a confirmation screen with the final price before submitting the order via POST /backups/order.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • multipart/form-data
  • application/json

Form parameters

validateOnly (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
serviceType (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null format: int32
coupon (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null

Return type

BackupOrderPutResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "continue" : true,
  "errors" : [ ],
  "serviceType" : 10831,
  "serviceCost" : "3.00",
  "originalCost" : "3.00",
  "repeatServiceCost" : "3.00",
  "hostname" : "",
  "password" : "m13dxi6K",
  "coupon" : "",
  "couponCode" : 0
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Validate Backup Order Response BackupOrderPutResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

Billing

Up 
post /account/creditcards
Add Credit Card to Account (addAccountCreditCard)
Adds a new credit card to the account for billing. Card details are validated and stored securely. The card may require verification before it can be used as a payment method.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • multipart/form-data
  • application/json

Form parameters

name (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
address (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
city (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
state (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
country (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
zip (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
cc (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
cc_exp (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
cc_ccv2 (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
post /billing/creditcards
Add Credit Card for Billing (addBillingCreditCard)
Adds a new credit card to the account for use as a payment method. If the card requires verification, the response indicates the next step. Complete verification via GET /billing/creditcards/{id}/verify followed by POST /billing/creditcards/{id}/verify before the card can be used for payments.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

Request body

BillingAddCcRequest BillingAddCcRequest (required)
Body Parameter —

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
post /billing/prepays
Create Prepay Deposit (addBillingPrepay)
Creates a new prepay balance deposit and returns the invoice ID that must be paid to activate it. The prepay balance can then be used as a payment method for future orders. Use /billing/invoices/{id} to retrieve the generated invoice details.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

Request body

BillingPrepayRequest BillingPrepayRequest (required)
Body Parameter —

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Up 
delete /account/creditcards/{id}
Remove Credit Card (deleteAccountCreditCard)
Removes a credit card from the account. If this is the default payment method, select a new default via /billing/payment_method afterward.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The credit card ID. Use the card ID returned from POST /account/creditcards or listed in /billing/creditcards. default: null

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

default

Default response
Up 
delete /billing/creditcards/{id}
Delete Credit Card (deleteBillingCreditCard)
Removes the selected credit card from the account. Use /billing/payment_method to select a new default payment method after deleting a card.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The credit card ID to remove. Use IDs from GET /billing/creditcards. default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
delete /billing/invoices/{id}
Delete Invoice (deleteBillingInvoice)
Deletes a pending (unpaid) invoice from the account. Only invoices with a pending payment status can be deleted. Paid invoices cannot be removed. Related service records and repeat invoices are also cleaned up.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The invoice ID to delete. Only unpaid invoices can be deleted. default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
delete /billing/prepays/{id}
Delete Prepay Balance (deleteBillingPrepay)
Deletes a prepay balance from the account. The balance must have remaining funds to be eligible for deletion. Use GET /billing/prepays to list available prepay balances and their IDs.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The prepay balance ID to delete. default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

get /affiliate/sales_graph
Get Affiliate Sales Graph Data (getAffiliateSalesGraph)
Returns time-series sales data for the requested number of days. Use this to render sales trend charts in an affiliate dashboard. Each data point represents aggregated sales for a time period.

Query parameters

days (optional)
Query Parameter — Number of days of sales history to include in the graph data. Determines the time window for the returned data points. default: null

Return type

StatusMonthlyBreakdown

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{ }

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Affiliate sales graph data broken down by time period. StatusMonthlyBreakdown

Up 
get /invoices
Get Invoices (getInvoices)
Returns a paginated list of invoices for the authenticated account. Each invoice includes the invoice number, date, total amount, and payment status. Use the optional searchString parameter to filter results and skip/limit for pagination.

Query parameters

searchString (optional)
Query Parameter — pass an optional search string for looking up inventory default: null
skip (optional)
Query Parameter — number of records to skip for pagination default: null format: int32
limit (optional)
Query Parameter — maximum number of records to return default: null format: int32

Return type

array[Invoice]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
[ {
  "id" : 0
}, {
  "id" : 0
} ]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

search results matching criteria

400

bad input parameter

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /pay/{method}/{invoices}
Initiate Payment (initiatePayment)
Initiates a payment for the specified invoices using the chosen payment method. The response type determines how your client should proceed: redirect means send the user to the provided URL, submit means POST a form with the provided fields, and single means the payment was processed immediately. Use invoice IDs obtained from order responses or /billing/invoices.

Path parameters

method (required)
Path Parameter — The payment method to use. Valid values: cc (credit card), paypal, prepay, payssion, payu, ccavenue, cashfree, coinbase, btcpay. default: null
invoices (required)
Path Parameter — A comma-separated list of invoice IDs to pay. These IDs are returned by order endpoints (e.g. /backups/order, /vps/order) and by /billing/invoices. default: null

Return type

initiatePayment_200_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "redirect" : "redirect",
  "method" : "method",
  "action" : "action",
  "text" : "text",
  "type" : "redirect",
  "items" : "{}"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Payment initiation response with redirect or form data. initiatePayment_200_response

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
post /billing/creditcards/{id}/verify
Submit Credit Card Verification (postBillingCreditCardVerify)
Completes the credit card verification flow by submitting the micro-charge amounts or CVV as required by GET /billing/creditcards/{id}/verify. A successful response means the card is verified and can be selected as a payment method via /billing/payment_method.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The credit card ID to verify. Use the ID returned from POST /billing/creditcards. default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

Request body

BillingVerifyCcRequest BillingVerifyCcRequest (required)
Body Parameter —

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

default

Default response

Updates the affiliate dock settings including the referral coupon and marketing copy. The dock is the branded landing page shown to visitors arriving via your affiliate link. Use this to customize the coupon code and promotional text.

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

DNS

Up 
post /dns
Create DNS Domain (addDnsDomain)
Creates a new DNS domain and assigns an initial A record pointing to the supplied IP address. The domain is immediately available on InterServer's DNS servers. Use /dns/{id} to manage records after creation.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • multipart/form-data
  • application/json

Form parameters

domain (required)
Form Parameter — The domain name. default: null
ip (required)
Form Parameter — IP Address to point the domain to. default: null

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

default

Default response
Up 
post /dns/{id}
Add DNS Record to Domain (addDnsRecord)
Adds a new DNS record to the specified domain. Provide the record type (A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, etc.), name, content, TTL, and priority. The record takes effect on the DNS servers immediately. Use GET /dns/{id} afterward to confirm the record was created.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The DNS Domain ID. default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • multipart/form-data
  • application/json

Form parameters

name (required)
Form Parameter — Name part of record default: null
type (required)
Form Parameter — default: null
content (required)
Form Parameter — Content of record default: null
ttl (optional)
Form Parameter — Time-to-live default: 86400 format: int32
prio (optional)
Form Parameter — Priority default: 0 format: int32

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Add DNS Domain Response

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
delete /dns/{id}
Delete DNS Domain (deleteDnsDomain)
Deletes a DNS domain and all of its associated records from the DNS servers. This action is permanent and cannot be undone. Any services relying on these DNS records will be affected immediately.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The DNS domain ID to delete. Use the id from GET /dns to identify the domain. default: null

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

default

Default response
Up 
delete /dns/{domainId}/{recordId}
Delete DNS Record (deleteDnsRecord)
Removes a DNS record from the specified domain. The deletion takes effect on the DNS servers immediately. Use GET /dns/{id} to verify the record has been removed.

Path parameters

domainId (required)
Path Parameter — The DNS domain ID. Use the id from GET /dns to identify the domain. default: null
recordId (required)
Path Parameter — The DNS record ID within the domain. Use the record id from GET /dns/{id} to identify the record. default: null

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

default

Default response
Up 
get /dns/{id}
List Domain DNS Records (getDnsDomain)
Returns the full set of DNS records for the specified domain, including NS, A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, and other record types. Use the record id values with /dns/{domainId}/{recordId} to update or delete individual records.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The DNS domain ID. Use the id from GET /dns to identify the domain. default: null

Return type

array[DnsRecord]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
[ {
  "id" : "473",
  "domain_id" : "68",
  "name" : "hussfamily.com",
  "type" : "NS",
  "content" : "cdns1.interserver.net",
  "ttl" : "86400",
  "prio" : "0",
  "disabled" : "0",
  "ordername" : "",
  "auth" : "1"
}, {
  "id" : "473",
  "domain_id" : "68",
  "name" : "hussfamily.com",
  "type" : "NS",
  "content" : "cdns1.interserver.net",
  "ttl" : "86400",
  "prio" : "0",
  "disabled" : "0",
  "ordername" : "",
  "auth" : "1"
} ]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

The DNS records for the specified domain.

Example data

Content-Type:

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /dns
List DNS Domains (getDnsList)
Returns the DNS domains on your account along with their primary A record content. Use the id from each entry with /dns/{id} to retrieve the full record set, or to add, update, and delete individual records.

Return type

array[DnsListItem]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
[ {
  "id" : 68,
  "name" : "hussfamily.com",
  "content" : "64.20.35.186"
}, {
  "id" : 68,
  "name" : "hussfamily.com",
  "content" : "64.20.35.186"
} ]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Listing of DNS domains on the account with their primary A record.

Example data

Content-Type:

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
post /dns/{domainId}/{recordId}
Update DNS Record (updateDnsRecord)
Updates an existing DNS record with new values. Use GET /dns/{id} to list records and retrieve the record IDs before updating. Changes propagate to the DNS servers immediately.

Path parameters

domainId (required)
Path Parameter — The DNS domain ID. Use the id from GET /dns to identify the domain. default: null
recordId (required)
Path Parameter — The DNS record ID within the domain. Use the record id from GET /dns/{id} to identify the record. default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • multipart/form-data
  • application/json

Form parameters

name (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
type (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
content (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
ttl (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
prio (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
disabled (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
ordername (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
auth (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

default

Default response

Adds DNSSEC DS records to the domain registration. Provide one or more DNSSEC record entries (algorithm, digest type, digest, key tag). Registrations must be active.

  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

Adds a new registered nameserver (glue record) for the domain using the supplied hostname and IP address. Use GET /domains/{id}/nameservers to confirm that the new entry exists.

id (required)

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
delete /domains/{id}
Cancel Domain Order (cancelDomain)
Cancels the domain order in the billing system. After cancellation the service will no longer auto-renew and will transition to a canceled status. The domain registration itself may remain active at the registrar until its current expiration date, but no further renewals will be processed. Treat the domain as inactive in downstream automation after this call succeeds.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The domain service ID. Use domain_id from GET /domains. default: null

Return type

CancelDomain_200_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Domains is canceled."
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Domains Cancel CancelDomain_200_response

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
delete /domains/{id}/dnssec

Removes DNSSEC DS records from the domain registration. Use this to disable DNSSEC at the registrar level.

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /domains/{id}
Get Domain Order (getDomainInfo)
Fetches the detailed domain service record (billing, registration, and service metadata) for the specified order ID. Use the domain_hostname from this response when calling /domains/lookup/{name} or when interacting with nameserver and DNSSEC management endpoints.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The domain service ID. Use domain_id from GET /domains. default: null

Return type

Domain

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "serviceType" : {
    "services_id" : "services_id",
    "services_category" : "services_category",
    "services_field1" : "services_field1",
    "services_field2" : "services_field2",
    "services_name" : "services_name",
    "services_type" : "services_type",
    "services_buyable" : "services_buyable",
    "services_cost" : "services_cost",
    "services_module" : "services_module"
  },
  "client_links" : [ {
    "link" : "link",
    "icon" : "icon",
    "label" : "label",
    "icon_text" : "icon_text",
    "help_text" : "help_text"
  }, {
    "link" : "link",
    "icon" : "icon",
    "label" : "label",
    "icon_text" : "icon_text",
    "help_text" : "help_text"
  } ],
  "billingDetails" : {
    "service_currency" : "service_currency",
    "service_extra" : {
      "zip" : "zip",
      "country" : "country",
      "firstname" : "firstname",
      "address" : "address",
      "address3" : "address3",
      "address2" : "address2",
      "city" : "city",
      "lastname" : "lastname",
      "domain_id" : "domain_id",
      "provProcessPending" : {
        "protocol" : "protocol",
        "response_code" : "response_code",
        "action" : "action",
        "response_text" : "response_text",
        "attributes" : {
          "registration expiration date" : "registration expiration date",
          "f_auto_renew" : "f_auto_renew",
          "id" : "id",
          "order_id" : "order_id"
        },
        "_OPS_version" : "_OPS_version",
        "is_success" : "is_success",
        "object" : "object"
      },
      "phone" : "phone",
      "company" : "company",
      "state" : "state",
      "fax" : "fax",
      "order_id" : "order_id",
      "email" : "email",
      "order" : {
        "protocol" : "protocol",
        "response_code" : "response_code",
        "action" : "action",
        "attributes" : {
          "id" : "id",
          "admin_email" : "admin_email"
        },
        "response_text" : "response_text",

Content-Type: application/json

  "code" : 0,

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Domain Information. Domain

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /domains/{id}/invoices
Get Domain Invoices (getDomainInvoices)
Retrieves invoice records associated with the domain order. Use this to reconcile billing or to confirm whether renewals or add-ons (such as Whois privacy) have been invoiced.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The domain service ID. Use domain_id from GET /domains. default: null

Return type

ChargeInvoiceRows

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "invoices" : {
    "1234565" : {
      "invoices_id" : 1234565,
      "invoices_description" : "Current IP + Scrub",
      "invoices_amount" : 5,
      "invoices_date" : "2025-12-05T19:38:24.000Z",
      "invoices_paid" : 1,
      "invoices_due_date" : "2025-12-19T19:38:24.000Z",
      "invoices_currency" : "USD",
      "currency_symbol" : "$",
      "invoices_date_formatted" : "5th Dec 2025",
      "paid_invoices" : {
        "654321" : {
          "invoices_id" : "654321,",
          "invoices_description" : "Credit Card Payment 2189347824",
          "invoices_amount" : 5,
          "invoices_date" : "2025-12-05T19:38:38.000Z",
          "invoices_currency" : "USD",
          "currency_symbol" : "$",
          "invoices_date_formatted" : "5th Dec 2025",
          "payment_type" : "Credit Card Payment",
          "refund_invoices" : {
            "98765" : {
              "invoices_id" : 98765,
              "invoices_description" : "REFUND: Credit Card Payment 2189347824",
              "invoices_amount" : 5,
              "invoices_date" : "2025-12-16T13:32:22.000Z",
              "invoices_currency" : "USD",
              "currency_symbol" : "$",
              "invoices_date_formatted" : "16th Dec 2025"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Get Invoices response ChargeInvoiceRows

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /domains/lookup/{name}
Lookup Domain Availability and Pricing (getDomainLookup)
Checks a domain for availability and premium status, and returns pricing information for new, renewal, and transfer actions when possible. The response also includes the order field metadata you can pass to /domains/order when placing a registration or transfer.

Path parameters

name (required)
Path Parameter — The full domain name to look up (for example example.com). default: null

Return type

DomainLookupResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "new" : "new",
  "website" : true,
  "domain_service" : true,
  "premium" : true,
  "transfer" : "transfer",
  "renewal" : "renewal",
  "service" : "{}",
  "available" : true,
  "whois_privacy" : true,
  "fields" : "{}",
  "currencies" : "{}"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "text" : "You were successfull."
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Availability, pricing, and field metadata for the requested domain. DomainLookupResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

422

The domain input was invalid. TextResponse

Example data

Content-Type:
Up 
get /domains/{id}/nameservers
List Registered Nameservers (getDomainNameservers)
Returns the registered nameserver hosts and glue IP addresses currently configured at the registrar. Use the returned list when adding, updating, or deleting individual nameserver records.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The domain service ID. Use domain_id from GET /domains. default: null

Return type

DomainNameserverGetResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
[ {
  "ipaddress" : "1.2.3.4",
  "can_delete" : "1",
  "name" : "ns1.domain.com"
}, {
  "ipaddress" : "1.2.3.4",
  "can_delete" : "1",
  "name" : "ns1.domain.com"
} ]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Domain registered nameservers list response DomainNameserverGetResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /domains/order/{domain}/{regType}
Get Domain Order Fields (getDomainOrderFields)
Returns the registration-type-specific form fields required to place a domain order for the given hostname. Use the returned fields to build the POST body for POST /domains/order.

Path parameters

domain (required)
Path Parameter — The fully qualified domain name (e.g. example.com). default: null
regType (required)
Path Parameter — The registration type. Common values include register for new registrations and transfer for inbound transfers. default: null

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Registration-type-specific form field definitions.

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /domains/order/{domain}
Get Domain Order Search Results (getDomainOrderSearchResults)
Returns order data for the specified domain hostname, including availability and pricing details. Use this to preview order information before placing a registration or transfer.

Path parameters

domain (required)
Path Parameter — The fully qualified domain name to look up (e.g. example.com). default: null

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Domain availability and pricing information.

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /domains/{id}/renew
Start Domain Renewal Flow (getDomainRenewal)
Starts the domain renewal workflow for an active domain. This endpoint currently returns a status message indicating that renewal handling has been triggered.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The domain service ID. Use domain_id from GET /domains. default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /domains/search/{name}
Search Domain Suggestions (getDomainSearch)
Queries the registrar for suggested domains and availability data. Use the returned lookup results to identify availability and then call /domains/lookup/{name} or /domains/order to obtain pricing and order fields for the chosen domain.

Path parameters

name (required)
Path Parameter — The base domain name to search (for example example or example.com). default: null

Return type

DomainSearchResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "lookup" : [ "{}", "{}" ],
  "tlds" : [ "tlds", "tlds" ],
  "success" : true,
  "response_text" : "response_text",
  "response_time" : "response_time",
  "suggest" : [ "{}", "{}" ]
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "text" : "You were successfull."
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Suggested and lookup results for the supplied search term. DomainSearchResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

404

No search suggestions or registrar response available. TextResponse

Example data

Content-Type:
Up 
get /domains/{id}/transfer
Start Domain Transfer Flow (getDomainTransfer)
Initiates the transfer workflow for a domain already in your account. Use this in coordination with /domains/{id}/contact to ensure registrant details are ready for the transfer request.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The domain service ID. Use domain_id from GET /domains. default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /domains/{id}/whois
Get Whois Privacy Status (getDomainWhoisPrivacy)
Returns status text for the Whois privacy add-on on the domain. Use this to determine whether privacy can be enabled or disabled for the current TLD before submitting updates.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The domain service ID. Use domain_id from GET /domains. default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /domains
List Domain Orders (getDomainsList)
Returns the domain services on your account, including the billing order ID, hostname, expiration date, and status. Use the domain_id from this response with GET /domains/{id} to fetch full service details, or with /domains/{id}/nameservers, /domains/{id}/contact, and /domains/{id}/whois to manage registration settings.

Return type

array[DomainRow]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
[ {
  "domain_id" : "592337",
  "domain_hostname" : "mydomain.com",
  "domain_expire_date" : "2023-08-14T00:59:38.000Z",
  "cost" : "18.00",
  "domain_status" : "active"
}, {
  "domain_id" : "592337",
  "domain_hostname" : "mydomain.com",
  "domain_expire_date" : "2023-08-14T00:59:38.000Z",
  "cost" : "18.00",
  "domain_status" : "active"
} ]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

The listing of Domains services on your account.

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

get /domains/{id}/welcome_email
Resend Domain Welcome Email (getDomainsWelcomeEmail)
Resends the welcome email for the domain service. The email contains registration details and management instructions.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The domain service ID. Use domain_id from GET /domains. default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Content-Type: application/json
{

  • application/json

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /domains/order
Get Domain Ordering Information (getNewDomain)
Returns the catalog of available domain services, including TLD service IDs and base Whois privacy pricing. Use the tldServices map to resolve a TLD into a service ID when you build an order request for /domains/order (POST) or the domain-field lookup endpoints.

Return type

DomainOrder

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "whoisPrivacyCost" : "5",
  "services" : {
    "DomainOrderServices10001" : {
      "services_id" : "10001",
      "services_category" : "100",
      "services_field1" : ".asia",
      "services_field2" : "",
      "services_name" : ".asia Domain Name Registration",
      "services_type" : "100",
      "services_currency" : "USD",
      "services_buyable" : "1",
      "services_cost" : "19.00",
      "services_hidden" : "0",
      "services_module" : "domains"
    }
  },
  "tldServices" : "{}"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Domain registration order information. DomainOrder

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
patch /domains/order
Validate Domain Order (patchDomains)
Returns the domain-specific field requirements for the supplied hostname and registration type. Use this to build the form data required for /domains/order (POST).

{

Request Domain Renewal (postDomainRenewal)
Submits a domain renewal request for the order. Use /domains/{id}/invoices to identify any invoices created as part of the renewal flow.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The domain service ID. Use domain_id from GET /domains. default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

Submits a transfer request for the domain order. Use /domains/{id}/invoices to identify any invoices generated as part of the transfer process.

id (required)

SuccessTextResponse

}

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

put /domains/order
Domain Order Search (putDomains)
Returns validation data for a domain order request (such as availability and pricing information). This is typically used as a pre-flight check before submitting a POST order.

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
post /domains/{id}/contact
Update Domain Contact Details (updateDomainContact)
Updates the registrant/admin contact details for the domain. Submit the same fields returned by the GET response after making the desired changes. Domain updates require the service to be active and unlocked.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The domain service ID. Use domain_id from GET /domains. default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

Request body

DomainContactDetails DomainContactDetails (required)
Body Parameter —

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
post /domains/{id}
Update Domain Order (updateDomainInfo)
Updates the domain service record for the order. Use this for account-level changes such as updating stored registration metadata or transfer attributes.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The domain service ID. Use domain_id from GET /domains. default: null

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

default

Default response
Up 
put /domains/{id}/nameservers
Replace Nameserver Set (updateDomainNameservers)
Replaces the full nameserver assignment for the domain with the provided list. This is the primary method for changing which authoritative nameservers the domain delegates to.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The domain service ID. Use domain_id from GET /domains. default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

Request body

DomainNameserverPutRequest DomainNameserverPutRequest (required)
Body Parameter —

Return type

TextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "text" : "You were successfull."
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Response with a text message field. TextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
post /domains/{id}/whois
Update Whois Privacy (updateDomainWhoisPrivacy)
Enables or disables the Whois privacy add-on for the domain. The API may create invoices when enabling privacy; use /domains/{id}/invoices to track billing events.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The domain service ID. Use domain_id from GET /domains. default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

Request body

DomainWhoisPrivacyRequest DomainWhoisPrivacyRequest (required)
Body Parameter —

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

FloatingIPs

Up 
post /floating_ips/order
Place Floating IP Order (addFloatingIp)
Places an order for a new Floating IP service. Use PUT /floating_ips/order to validate the order first.

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

default

Default response
Up 
delete /floating_ips/{id}
Cancel Floating IP (floatingIpsCancel)
Cancels a Floating IP service. After cancellation the IP assignment is released and the service transitions to a canceled status. No further billing charges will be incurred.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The Floating IP service ID. Use the ID from GET /floating_ips. default: null

Return type

floating_ipsCancel_200_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Floating IPs is canceled."
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Floating Ip Cancel floating_ipsCancel_200_response

id (required)

          "invoices_description" : "Credit Card Payment 2189347824",

      }
    }
  }

post /mail/{id}/alerts
Create Mail Alert (createMailAlert)
Creates a new alert for the mail service, such as delivery or quota notifications.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The mail service ID. Use mail_id from GET /mail. default: null

  • application/json

MailAlertRequest MailAlertRequest (required)

SuccessTextResponse

id (required)

ChargeInvoiceRows

  "success" : true,
  "invoices" : {
    "1234565" : {

      "invoices_description" : "Current IP + Scrub",

      "invoices_date" : "2025-12-05T19:38:24.000Z",

          "invoices_description" : "Credit Card Payment 2189347824",

              "currency_symbol" : "$",

  "sent" : 495,

      "client@anothersite.com" : 9

post /mail/{id}/send

id (required)

Default response
Up 
get /mail/{id}/log
View Mail Log (viewMailLog)

Returns a paginated log of emails sent through this mail service, with optional filtering by sender, recipient, date range, and delivery status.

Row grouping is controlled by the groupby parameter. By default (groupby=recipient), the response contains one row per delivery attempt — so a single message sent to 4 recipients produces 4 rows, each with its own recipient, delivered, response, and mxHostname values. Set groupby=message to collapse to one row per message (delivery fields will reflect one arbitrary recipient).

Pagination is controlled by skip and limit. The total in the response reflects the row count after grouping, so it matches the number of pages you need to fetch.

Date filtering accepts either a Unix timestamp (integer) or a date string parseable by PHP strtotime() such as 2024-01-15, last monday, or 2024-01-01 00:00:00. Examples: startDate=1704067200&endDate=1706745599 or startDate=2024-01-01&endDate=2024-01-31.

Sorting is controlled by sort and dir. Currently the only sort key is time (default), which orders by internal row ID.

Delivery status can be filtered with the delivered parameter: delivered=1 returns only successfully delivered messages; delivered=0 returns messages still in queue or that failed.

Address filtering distinguishes between the SMTP envelope address (from, to) and message headers (headerfrom for the From: header, replyto for Reply-To:). These may differ when a message is sent on behalf of another address.

The mailid parameter corresponds to the id field in the returned MailLogEntry objects, not the _id field. It also matches the transaction ID returned in the text field of a successful send response.

The messageId parameter searches the Message-ID email header (case-insensitive substring match).

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The mail service ID. Use mail_id from GET /mail. default: null

Query parameters

id (optional)
Query Parameter — The numeric ID of the mail order to filter by. When omitted, logs from the first active mail order are returned. Obtain valid IDs from GET /mail or GET /mail/{id}. default: null format: int64
origin (optional)
Query Parameter — Filter by the originating IP address from which the message was submitted to the relay. Must be a valid IPv4 or IPv6 address. default: null format: ipv4
mx (optional)
Query Parameter — Filter by the MX hostname the relay attempted delivery to. For example mx.google.com would return messages destined for Gmail recipients. Maps to mxHostname in the MailLogEntry response. default: null
from (optional)
Query Parameter — Filter by SMTP envelope MAIL FROM address (exact match). This is the address the relay used for bounce handling and may differ from the From: message header. For header-level filtering use headerfrom. default: null format: email
to (optional)
Query Parameter — Filter by SMTP envelope RCPT TO address (exact match). This is the delivery address used by the relay and may differ from the To: header when BCC recipients are involved. default: null format: email
subject (optional)
Query Parameter — Filter by email Subject header (exact match). MIME-encoded subjects are decoded automatically in the response. default: null
mailid (optional)
Query Parameter — Filter by the relay-assigned mail ID string (exact match). This corresponds to the id field in MailLogEntry and to the text value returned by the sending endpoints on success. Format is an 18-19 character hexadecimal string such as 185997065c60008840. default: null
messageId (optional)
Query Parameter — Filter by the Message-ID email header using a substring (case-insensitive) match. The Message-ID is assigned by the sending mail client and is visible in the messageId field of MailLogEntry. default: null
replyto (optional)
Query Parameter — Filter by the Reply-To message header address (exact match). Only returns messages where this header was explicitly set. default: null format: email
headerfrom (optional)
Query Parameter — Filter by the From message header address (exact match). This is the human-visible sender address and may differ from the SMTP envelope from parameter when sending on behalf of another address. default: null format: email
delivered (optional)
Query Parameter — Filter by delivery status. 1 returns only messages that were successfully delivered to the destination MX. 0 returns messages that are still queued, deferred, or failed. Omit to return all messages regardless of delivery status. default: null
skip (optional)
Query Parameter — Number of records to skip for pagination. Use in combination with limit to page through large result sets. Defaults to 0 (no skip). default: 0 format: int32
limit (optional)
Query Parameter — Maximum number of records to return per page. Defaults to 100. Maximum allowed value is 10000. The response also includes a total field with the full matched count so you can calculate the number of pages. default: 100 format: int32
startDate (optional)
Query Parameter — Earliest date to include. Accepts either a Unix timestamp (integer seconds since epoch) or a date string parseable by strtotime() such as 2024-01-15 or last monday. Messages with a time value greater than or equal to this value will be included. default: null
endDate (optional)
Query Parameter — Latest date to include. Accepts either a Unix timestamp (integer seconds since epoch) or a date string parseable by strtotime() such as 2024-01-31 or yesterday. Messages with a time value less than or equal to this value will be included. default: null
sort (optional)
Query Parameter — Field to sort results by. Currently only time is supported (sorts by internal row ID which corresponds to chronological order). default: time
dir (optional)
Query Parameter — Sort direction. desc returns newest first (default), asc returns oldest first. default: desc
groupby (optional)
Query Parameter — Controls how results are grouped. recipient (default) returns one row per delivery attempt — a message sent to 4 recipients produces 4 rows, each with its own recipient, delivered, response, and delivery metadata. message collapses to one row per unique message ID; delivery-level fields will reflect one arbitrary recipient per message. The total count in the response matches the grouping mode. default: recipient

Return type

MailLog

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "total" : 1,
  "skip" : 0,
  "limit" : 100,
  "emails" : [ {
    "_id" : 103172,
    "id" : "17c7eda538e0005d03",
    "from" : "person@mysite.com",
    "to" : "client@isp.com",
    "subject" : "sell 0.005 shares",
    "messageId" : "",
    "created" : "2021-10-14 08:50:10",
    "time" : 1634215809,
    "user" : "mb5658",
    "transtype" : "ESMTPSA",
    "origin" : "199.231.189.154",
    "interface" : "feeder",
    "sendingZone" : "interserver",
    "bodySize" : 63,
    "seq" : 1,
    "delivered" : 1,
    "code" : 250,
    "recipient" : "client@isp.com",
    "domain" : "interserver.net",
    "locked" : 1,
    "lockTime" : "1634215818533",
    "assigned" : "relay1",
    "queued" : "2021-10-14T12:50:15.487Z",
    "mxHostname" : "mx.j.is.cc",
    "response" : "250 2.0.0 Ok queued as C91D83E128C"
  } ]
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Paginated list of mail log entries matching the specified filters. MailLog

Query Parameter — Get countries by iso2 or iso3 or numcode default: null

get /info
Get Server Info (getInfo)
Returns metadata about the API server's available modules, service packages, categories, and types. This public endpoint does not require authentication and is useful for discovering supported services before placing orders.

Return type

ServicesInfo

{
  "modules" : {
    "domains" : {

Returns the OAuth redirect URL for the specified provider. Redirect the user to this URL to begin the OAuth authentication flow. After the user authorizes, the provider will redirect back to your callback endpoint where you should call POST /oauth.

getOauthRedirect_200_response

}

{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.

200

OAuth redirect URL for the requested provider. getOauthRedirect_200_response

post /oauth
OAuth Callback (postOauthCallback)
Handles the OAuth callback after the user authorizes with the provider. Returns the login status. If the account has two-factor authentication enabled, the response will include error_code: "2fa_required" and an account_id; use PATCH /oauth with the 2FA code and account ID to complete login.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

postOauthCallback_request postOauthCallback_request (optional)
