provider (required)
Query Parameter — The OAuth provider name (e.g. Google). default: null

Return type

postOauthCallback_200_response

{
  "account_id" : 0,
  "error_code" : "error_code",
  "login" : true,
  "signup" : true,
  "linked" : true
}

  "code" : 0,

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

OAuth callback result. postOauthCallback_200_response

post /login
Submit Login Information (submitLogin)
Authenticates using the provided login credentials and returns a session token. Include the returned session ID in the sessionid header on subsequent requests. If authentication fails, a 402 error is returned.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • multipart/form-data
  • application/json

Form parameters

login (required)
Form Parameter — default: null
passwd (required)
Form Parameter — default: null
remember (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
g-recaptcha-response (optional)
Form Parameter — default: null
tfa (optional)
Form Parameter — Two Factor Authentication Response. default: null

Return type

LoginSuccessResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "sessionId" : "zzzzzzzzzzzzzz",
  "account_id" : 123,
  "account_lid" : "my@user.com",
  "ima" : "client",
  "gravatar" : "https://gravatar.com/user/image.png"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "message" : "missing 2fa",
  "field" : "2fa"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Successful login response containing the session token. LoginSuccessResponse

402

Error response to a login request. LoginErrorResponse

default

id (required)

Query parameters

all (optional)

file (required)
Query Parameter — The backup filename to delete. default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

  "code" : 0,

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /qs/{id}/block_smtp
Block QuickServer SMTP (doQsBlockSmtp)
Blocks outbound SMTP for the QuickServer to prevent email abuse. Use this action when responding to abuse notifications or to enforce outbound email policies.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — QuickServer ID number. default: null

Return type

QueueResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "text" : "Action has been sent to the server. Please allow up to 2 minutes for action to be completed.",
  "queueId" : 14670065
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Response message from sending a service queue. QueueResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
patch /qs/{id}/backups
Download QuickServer Backup (downloadQsBackup)
Generates a pre-signed download URL for the specified backup file. The URL is valid for 24 hours. Use GET /qs/{id}/backups to list available backup filenames.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — QuickServer ID number default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

Request body

downloadQsBackup_request downloadQsBackup_request (required)
Body Parameter —

Query parameters

all (optional)
Query Parameter — Set to 1 to list all backups across all services, not just the ones for the given QuickServer. default: null

Return type

downloadQsBackup_200_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "text" : "text",
  "url" : "url"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Download URL for the backup file. downloadQsBackup_200_response

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /qs/order
Get QuickServer Ordering Information (getNewQs)
Returns QuickServer ordering metadata and available plans. Use these details to build the order form and to validate a plan selection.

Return type

QuickserverOrder

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "server_details" : {
    "381" : {
      "cores" : 24,
      "cost" : "$140.00",
      "cpu" : "AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12-Core Processor",
      "hd" : "1760GB",
      "ram" : "119GB"
    }
  },
  "distro_sel" : {
    "Ubuntu" : {
      "Ubuntu" : "Ubuntu"
    }
  },
  "qs_id" : "221",
  "templates" : {
    "Ubuntu" : [ [ "ubuntu24", "ubuntu24" ], [ "ubuntu24", "ubuntu24" ] ]
  },
  "version" : {
    "centosstream-8" : {
      "centosstream-8" : "8 Stream (64 bits)"
    }
  }
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Quickserver Ordering information. QuickserverOrder

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /qs/{id}/backups
List QuickServer Backups (getQsBackups)
Returns the available backups for the QuickServer across all storage systems (Swift, MinIO, ZFS). Use the backup name value with PATCH /qs/{id}/backups to download or DELETE /qs/{id}/backups to remove a backup. Use POST /qs/{id}/restore to restore from a backup.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — QuickServer ID number default: null

Query parameters

all (optional)
Query Parameter — Set to 1 to list all backups across all services, not just the ones for the given QuickServer. default: null

Return type

VpsBackupRows

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
[ {
  "type" : "zfs",
  "service" : 2722890,
  "name" : "first",
  "size" : 3229615,
  "date" : 1694082660
}, {
  "type" : "swift",
  "service" : 100,
  "name" : "reset_shot_vps100_20210928.jpg",
  "size" : 40621,
  "date" : 1632842251
}, {
  "type" : "swift",
  "service" : 100,
  "name" : "reset_shot_vps100_20210929.jpg",
  "size" : 36319,
  "date" : 1632968243
} ]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

The listing of available backups for the QuickServer. VpsBackupRows

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

get /qs/{id}

Returns detailed QuickServer information, including credentials, IPs, and available client actions.

id (required)
Path Parameter — QuickServer ID number. default: null

Quickserver

{

get /qs
List QuickServers (getQsList)
Returns the QuickServer services on your account. Use the qs_id values with /qs/{id} for details or with the action endpoints (restart, backup, etc.) to manage each server.

Return type

array[QuickserverRow]

Content-Type: application/json
[ {
  "qs_id" : "19504",
  "qs_name" : "Quickserver199",
  "cost" : "65.00",
  "qs_hostname" : "qs19504",
  "qs_status" : "canceled",
  "qs_comment" : ""

  "cost" : "65.00",
  "qs_hostname" : "qs19504",
  "qs_status" : "canceled",
  "qs_comment" : ""

ChargeInvoiceRows

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "invoices" : {
    "1234565" : {
      "invoices_id" : 1234565,
      "invoices_description" : "Current IP + Scrub",
      "invoices_amount" : 5,
      "invoices_date" : "2025-12-05T19:38:24.000Z",
      "invoices_paid" : 1,
      "invoices_due_date" : "2025-12-19T19:38:24.000Z",
      "invoices_currency" : "USD",
      "currency_symbol" : "$",
      "invoices_date_formatted" : "5th Dec 2025",
      "paid_invoices" : {
        "654321" : {
          "invoices_id" : "654321,",
          "invoices_description" : "Credit Card Payment 2189347824",
          "invoices_amount" : 5,
          "invoices_date" : "2025-12-05T19:38:38.000Z",
          "invoices_currency" : "USD",
          "currency_symbol" : "$",
          "invoices_date_formatted" : "5th Dec 2025",
          "payment_type" : "Credit Card Payment",
          "refund_invoices" : {
            "98765" : {
              "invoices_id" : 98765,
              "invoices_description" : "REFUND: Credit Card Payment 2189347824",
              "invoices_amount" : 5,
              "invoices_date" : "2025-12-16T13:32:22.000Z",
              "invoices_currency" : "USD",
              "currency_symbol" : "$",
              "invoices_date_formatted" : "16th Dec 2025"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

post /scrub_ips/{id}/create_filter
Create Traffic Filter (createFilter)
Creates a traffic filter for the Scrub IP service. Filters apply predefined scrubbing profiles (e.g., DNS, HTTP) to specific destination ports. Use GET /scrub_ips/filter_types to list available filter types.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — ScrubIp ID number default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json

Request body

CreateFilter CreateFilter (required)
Body Parameter —

Return type

createFilter_201_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "New filter has been created."
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : false,
  "text" : "Bad Request",
  "errors" : [ "Filter type is invalid", "Filter type is invalid" ]
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : false,
  "text" : "Unable to create new filter."
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

201

Request OK createFilter_201_response

400

Bad Request createFilter_400_response

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

500

Internal Server Error createFilter_500_response
Up 
post /scrub_ips/{id}/create_geo_rule

Creates a geographic-based firewall rule for the Scrub IP service. Geo rules allow you to block or allow traffic from specific countries or regions.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — ScrubIp ID number default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json

  "success" : true,
  "text" : "New firewall Rule has been created."
}

Example data

{
  "success" : false,
  "text" : "Bad Request",
  "errors" : [ "Country or Asn is required.", "Country or Asn is required." ]
}

Example data

{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json

  "success" : false,
  "text" : "Unable to create new firewall Rule."
}

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

500

Internal Server Error createRule_500_response
Up 
post /scrub_ips/{id}/create_rule
Create Firewall Rule (createRule)
Creates a new firewall rule for the Scrub IP service. Rules allow you to block or allow traffic based on source IP, destination port, and protocol.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — ScrubIp ID number default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json

Request body

CreateFirewallRule CreateFirewallRule (required)
Body Parameter —

Return type

createRule_201_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "New firewall Rule has been created."
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : false,
  "text" : "Bad Request",
  "errors" : [ "Source IP is not valid", "Source IP is not valid" ]
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : false,
  "text" : "Unable to create new firewall Rule."
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

201

Create firewall rule for scrub ip createRule_201_response

400

Bad Request createRule_400_response

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

500

Internal Server Error createRule_500_response
Up

id (required)

get /scrub_ips/{id}/disable
Disable Scrub Protection (disableScrub)
Disables DDoS scrubbing protection on the IP address. Traffic will no longer be routed through the scrubbing infrastructure.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — ScrubIp ID number default: null

Return type

disableScrub_200_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Scrub is disabled on your IP."
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : false,
  "text" : "Scrub is not enabled in this service."
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : false,
  "text" : "Unable to disable scrub on your IP."
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Request OK disableScrub_200_response

400

Bad request disableScrub_400_response

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

500

Internal Server Error disableScrub_500_response
Up 
get /scrub_ips/{id}/enable
Enable Scrub Protection (enableScrub)
Enables DDoS scrubbing protection on the IP address associated with this service. Traffic will be routed through the scrubbing infrastructure to filter malicious packets.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — ScrubIp ID number default: null

Return type

enableScrub_200_response

  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

500

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /servers/{id}
Get Server Order (getServerInfo)
Returns detailed information about a specific server including its hardware configuration, IPs, and status.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — Server ID number. default: null

Return type

Server

  "serviceExtra" : [ "serviceExtra", "serviceExtra" ],

Server details Server

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /servers/{id}/invoices
Get Server Invoices (getServerInvoices)
Returns the billing invoices associated with this dedicated server.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — Server ID number default: null

Return type

ChargeInvoiceRows

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "invoices" : {
    "1234565" : {
      "invoices_id" : 1234565,
      "invoices_description" : "Current IP + Scrub",
      "invoices_amount" : 5,
      "invoices_date" : "2025-12-05T19:38:24.000Z",
      "invoices_paid" : 1,
      "invoices_due_date" : "2025-12-19T19:38:24.000Z",
      "invoices_currency" : "USD",
      "currency_symbol" : "$",
      "invoices_date_formatted" : "5th Dec 2025",
      "paid_invoices" : {
        "654321" : {
          "invoices_id" : "654321,",
          "invoices_description" : "Credit Card Payment 2189347824",
          "invoices_amount" : 5,
          "invoices_date" : "2025-12-05T19:38:38.000Z",
          "invoices_currency" : "USD",
          "currency_symbol" : "$",
          "invoices_date_formatted" : "5th Dec 2025",
          "payment_type" : "Credit Card Payment",
          "refund_invoices" : {
            "98765" : {
              "invoices_id" : 98765,
              "invoices_description" : "REFUND: Credit Card Payment 2189347824",
              "invoices_amount" : 5,
              "invoices_date" : "2025-12-16T13:32:22.000Z",
              "invoices_currency" : "USD",
              "currency_symbol" : "$",
              "invoices_date_formatted" : "16th Dec 2025"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Get Invoices response ChargeInvoiceRows

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /servers
List Servers (getServerList)
Returns all dedicated server services on the account with their current status and configuration.

Return type

array[ServerRow]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
[ {
  "server_id" : "8404",
  "account_lid" : "detain@interserver.net",
  "server_hostname" : "testsignup.is.net",
  "server_status" : "deleted"
}, {
  "server_id" : "8404",
  "account_lid" : "detain@interserver.net",
  "server_hostname" : "testsignup.is.net",
  "server_status" : "deleted"
} ]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

The listing of Servers services on your account.

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /servers/{id}/reverse_dns
Reverse DNS Info (getServerReverseDns)
Returns the current reverse DNS (PTR record) entries for the server's IP addresses.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — Server ID number default: null

Return type

ReverseDnsEntries

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "ips" : {
    "1.2.3.4" : "yourhost.com",
    "1.2.3.5" : "anotherhost.com"
  }
}

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Servers Reverse DNS info response ReverseDnsEntries

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /servers/{id}/welcome_email
Resend Server Welcome Email (getServersWelcomeEmail)
Resends the welcome email for the order.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — Server ID number default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
post /servers/order/buy_now_server
Place Buy Now Server Order (placeBuyNowServer)
Places an order for a buy-it-now dedicated server. Use GET /servers/order/buy_now_server to retrieve available server configurations and their IDs before ordering.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json

Request body

placeBuyNowServer_request placeBuyNowServer_request (optional)
Body Parameter —

Return type

ServersBuyNowResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Server order is placed.",
  "order_details" : {
    "service_id" : 1234,
    "invoice_id" : 2342355
  }
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : false,
  "text" : "Unable to place order.",
  "errors" : [ "Server ID is missing.", "Server Hostname is missing.;", "Password must contain atleast 8 characters, one lowercase letter, one uppercase letter, one number & one special character" ]
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Order placed successfully. ServersBuyNowResponse

400

Order validation failed. ServersBuyNowError

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

default

Default response

Up 
post /servers/{id}/ipmi_live
Server IPMI Live Setup (serverIpmiLivePost)
Configures IPMI live access by whitelisting your current IP address for connections to the server's IPMI management interface.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — Server ID number default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • multipart/form-data
  • application/json

Form parameters

asset (optional)
Form Parameter — Asset ID default: null format: int32
ip (required)
Form Parameter — Your IP Address you wish to connect to the IPMI system from. default: null

Return type

ServerIpmiLiveInfo

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "text" : "Setup Complete",
  "public_ip" : "1.2.3.4",
  "allowed_ip" : "1.2.3.4",
  "client_username" : "user",
  "client_password" : "pass"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Response from the Servers IPMI Live information request. ServerIpmiLiveInfo

404

The specified resource was not found getAccountInfo_401_response

id (required)
Path Parameter — Server ID number default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • multipart/form-data
  • application/json

asset (optional)
Form Parameter — The Asset ID default: null format: int32
action (required)

delete /servers/{id}
Cancel Server Service (serversCancel)
Cancels the dedicated server service. The server will be deprovisioned and billing will stop at the end of the current billing cycle.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — Server ID number default: null

Return type

serversCancel_200_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Servers is canceled."
}

  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

  • application/json

200

Tickets

Up 
post /tickets/new
Create New Ticket (addNewTicket)
Creates a new support ticket. Optionally link it to a specific service by providing the service ID and module.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

Request body

TicketNew TicketNew (required)
Body Parameter —

Return type

TicketNewResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ticket is created!",
  "ticket_id" : 1759653
}

{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ticket is created!",
  "ticket_id" : 1759653
}

  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"

400

A successful response after creating a ticket TicketNewResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /tickets/{id}/close
Close Ticket (closeTicket)
Closes the specified support ticket. Closed tickets can still be viewed but will no longer appear in the active inbox.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — Ticket ID default: null

Return type

CloseTicketResponseSchema

{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ticket is closed!"
}

Example data

{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

default

Default response
Up 
get /tickets
List Support Tickets (getTicketsList)
Returns a paginated list of support tickets on the account. Filter by status and time period.

Query parameters

page (optional)
Query Parameter — Page number for paginated results. default: 1
period (optional)
Query Parameter — How far back to show tickets from. Value is in days. default: 30
view (optional)
Query Parameter — The status of tickets to view. Possible values are Open, Closed, On Hold, and In Progress. If not specified it will show all types. default: null

Return type

Tickets

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "ima" : "client",
  "custid" : "223513",
  "view" : "Open",
  "currentPage" : 1,
  "limit" : 50,
  "sortcol" : 6,
  "sortdir" : 1,
  "rowsOffset" : 0,
  "tickets" : [ ],
  "pages" : 7,
  "rowsTotal" : 311,
  "inboxCount" : 311,
  "countArray" : {
    "Open" : 3,
    "On Hold" : 3,
    "Closed" : 305
  },
  "viewText" : "Inbox"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

The listing of support tickets. Tickets

Example data

Content-Type:

Up 
post /vps/order
Place VPS Order (addVps)
Places an order for a new VPS. Use PUT /vps/order to validate the order first.

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

Request body

VpsOrderPostRequest VpsOrderPostRequest (optional)
Body Parameter —

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response

default

Default response
Up 
delete /vps/{id}/backups
Delete VPS Backup (deleteVpsBackup)
Permanently removes the specified backup file from storage. Use GET /vps/{id}/backups to list available backup filenames before deleting.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — VPS ID number default: null

Query parameters

all (optional)
Query Parameter — Set to 1 to list all backups across all services, not just the ones for the given VPS. default: null
file (required)
Query Parameter — The backup filename to delete. default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /vps/{id}/block_smtp
Blocks SMTP (doVpsBlockSmtp)
Blocks outbound SMTP (port 25) on the VPS to prevent spam. Useful for VPS that do not need to send email.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — VPS ID number. default: null

Return type

QueueResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "text" : "Action has been sent to the server. Please allow up to 2 minutes for action to be completed.",
  "queueId" : 14670065
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Response message from sending a service queue. QueueResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
patch /vps/{id}/backups
Download VPS Backup (downloadVpsBackup)
Generates a pre-signed download URL for the specified backup file. The URL is valid for 24 hours. Use GET /vps/{id}/backups to list available backup filenames.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — VPS ID number default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

Request body

downloadQsBackup_request downloadQsBackup_request (required)
Body Parameter —

Query parameters

all (optional)
Query Parameter — Set to 1 to list all backups across all services, not just the ones for the given VPS. default: null

Return type

downloadQsBackup_200_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "text" : "text",
  "url" : "url"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Download URL for the backup file. downloadQsBackup_200_response

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /vps/order
VPS Ordering Information (getNewVps)
Retrieves available VPS configurations, OS templates, and pricing for ordering.

Return type

VpsOrder

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "vpsSliceOvzCost" : 6,
  "bwType" : "2",
  "vpsSliceXenCost" : 6,
  "maxSlices" : "16",
  "vpsSliceHypervCost" : 10,
  "hdSlice" : "30",
  "bwSlice" : "2000",
  "platformNames" : {
    "kvm" : "KVM",
    "kvmstorage" : "KVM Storage",
    "hyperv" : "HyperV"
  },
  "vpsSliceSsdOvzCost" : 9,
  "osNames" : {
    "ubuntu" : "Ubuntu",
    "opensuse" : "OpenSUSE"
  },
  "vpsNyCost" : 3,
  "vpsSliceLxcCost" : 6,
  "ramSlice" : "2048",
  "hdStorageSlice" : "1000",
  "serviceTypes" : {
    "32" : {
      "services_id" : "32",
      "services_category" : "2",
      "services_field1" : "slice",
      "services_field2" : "",
      "services_name" : "KVM Windows VPS Slice",
      "services_type" : "1",
      "services_buyable" : "1",
      "services_cost" : "10.00",
      "services_module" : "vps"
    }
  },
  "currency" : "USD",
  "vpsSliceKvmStorageCost" : 6,
  "vpsSliceVirtuozzoCost" : 6,
  "locationStock" : {
    "1" : {
      "kvm" : true,
      "kvmstorage" : true,
      "hyperv" : true
    }
  },
  "locationNames" : {
    "3" : "Equinix NY4"
  },
  "vpsSliceKvmWCost" : 10,
  "templates" : {
    "hyperv" : {
      "windows" : {
        "Windows2022" : "2022",
        "Windows2019Standard" : "2019 Standard"
      }
    }
  },
  "bwTotal" : 2,
  "currencySymbol" : "$",
  "cpanelCost" : 22,
  "platformPackages" : {
    "kvm" : 32,
    "kvmstorage" : 57,
    "hyperv" : 54
  },
  "daCost" : 8,
  "packageCosts" : {
    "57" : 6
  },
  "vpsSliceKvmLCost" : 6,
  "vpsSliceSsdVirtuozzoCost" : 9,
  "vpsSliceVmwareCost" : 10

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

VPS Order information VpsOrder

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /vps/{id}/backups
Get VPS Backups List (getVpsBackups)
Returns the available backups for the VPS across all storage systems (Swift, MinIO, ZFS). Use the backup name value with PATCH /vps/{id}/backups to download or DELETE /vps/{id}/backups to remove a backup. Use POST /vps/{id}/restore to restore from a backup.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — VPS ID number default: null

Query parameters

all (optional)
Query Parameter — Set to 1 to list all backups across all services, not just the ones for the given VPS. default: null

Return type

VpsBackupRows

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
[ {
  "type" : "zfs",
  "service" : 2722890,
  "name" : "first",
  "size" : 3229615,
  "date" : 1694082660
}, {
  "type" : "swift",
  "service" : 100,
  "name" : "reset_shot_vps100_20210928.jpg",
  "size" : 40621,
  "date" : 1632842251
}, {
  "type" : "swift",
  "service" : 100,
  "name" : "reset_shot_vps100_20210929.jpg",
  "size" : 36319,
  "date" : 1632968243
} ]

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

The listing of the available backups VpsBackupRows

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /vps/{id}/invoices
Get VPS Invoices (getVpsInvoices)
Returns the billing invoices associated with this VPS.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — VPS ID number default: null

Return type

ChargeInvoiceRows

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "invoices" : {
    "1234565" : {
      "invoices_id" : 1234565,
      "invoices_description" : "Current IP + Scrub",
      "invoices_amount" : 5,

              "currency_symbol" : "$",

            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

get /vps/{id}/traffic_usage

Returns bandwidth traffic usage data for the VPS.

id (required)
Path Parameter — VPS ID number default: null

Return type

VpsTrafficResponse

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "name" : "vps2725349",
  "target" : "2725349",
  "interval" : 30,
  "history" : {
    "hour" : {
      "data" : [ [ "2023-09-11T12:00:00Z", 834424 ], [ "2023-09-11T23:00:00Z", 8247944 ], [ "2023-09-14T05:00:00Z", 19763064 ], [ "2023-09-14T06:00:00Z", 18892320 ], [ "2023-09-17T07:00:00Z", 16546448 ] ],

}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up

id (required)

QueueResponse

post /vps/{id}/restore
Restore VPS from Backup (postVpsRestore)
Initiates a restore of the VPS from a previously created backup. The restore operation overwrites the current disk contents. Use GET /vps/{id}/backups to retrieve available backup names.

id (required)
Path Parameter — VPS ID number default: null

  "custCurrency" : "USD"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Website details Website

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /websites/{id}/invoices
Get Website Invoices (getWebsiteInvoices)
Returns the billing invoices associated with this webhosting service.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The website service ID. Use website_id from GET /websites. default: null

Return type

ChargeInvoiceRows

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "invoices" : {
    "1234565" : {
      "invoices_id" : 1234565,
      "invoices_description" : "Current IP + Scrub",
      "invoices_amount" : 5,
      "invoices_date" : "2025-12-05T19:38:24.000Z",
      "invoices_paid" : 1,
      "invoices_due_date" : "2025-12-19T19:38:24.000Z",
      "invoices_currency" : "USD",
      "currency_symbol" : "$",
      "invoices_date_formatted" : "5th Dec 2025",
      "paid_invoices" : {
        "654321" : {
          "invoices_id" : "654321,",
          "invoices_description" : "Credit Card Payment 2189347824",
          "invoices_amount" : 5,
          "invoices_date" : "2025-12-05T19:38:38.000Z",
          "invoices_currency" : "USD",
          "currency_symbol" : "$",
          "invoices_date_formatted" : "5th Dec 2025",
          "payment_type" : "Credit Card Payment",
          "refund_invoices" : {
            "98765" : {
              "invoices_id" : 98765,
              "invoices_description" : "REFUND: Credit Card Payment 2189347824",
              "invoices_amount" : 5,
              "invoices_date" : "2025-12-16T13:32:22.000Z",
              "invoices_currency" : "USD",
              "currency_symbol" : "$",
              "invoices_date_formatted" : "16th Dec 2025"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Get Invoices response ChargeInvoiceRows

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /websites

array[WebsiteRow]

  "repeat_invoices_cost" : "5.99",

  "name" : "name"
}, {

Up 
get /websites/{id}/login
Hosting Panel Auto Login (getWebsitesLogin)
Returns an auto-login URL for the webhosting control panel. Use this to access cPanel or DirectAdmin without entering credentials.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The website service ID. Use website_id from GET /websites. default: null

Return type

WebsiteLoginResponse

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "type" : "location",
  "location" : "https://www.site.com/"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

  • application/json

Responses

200

Get Website Login response WebsiteLoginResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /websites/{id}/welcome_email
Resend Website Welcome Email (getWebsitesWelcomeEmail)
Resends the welcome email containing hosting credentials and panel access details for the webhosting order.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The website service ID. Use website_id from GET /websites. default: null

Return type

SuccessTextResponse

Example data

{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Ok"
}

{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.

200

A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message. SuccessTextResponse

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
get /websites/{id}/reverse_dns
Get Website Reverse DNS (gettWebsiteReverseDns)
Returns the current reverse DNS (PTR record) entries for the website's IP addresses.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The website service ID. Use website_id from GET /websites. default: null

Return type

ReverseDnsEntries

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "ips" : {
    "1.2.3.4" : "yourhost.com",
    "1.2.3.5" : "anotherhost.com"
  }
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

List of reverse dns entries ReverseDnsEntries

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
post /websites/{id}/buy_ip
Update Website IP DNS (postWebsiteBuyIp)
Updates the reverse DNS hostnames for the website's IP addresses. Provide an ips object mapping each IP address to its desired hostname.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The website service ID. Use website_id from GET /websites. default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

Request body

postWebsiteBuyIp_request postWebsiteBuyIp_request (required)
Body Parameter —

Return type

postWebsiteBuyIp_200_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "message" : "message"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

DNS update result. postWebsiteBuyIp_200_response

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
post /websites/{id}/migration
Request Website Migration (postWebsiteMigration)
Submits a website migration request from your current hosting provider to InterServer. Provide the credentials and details for your current host so our team can perform the migration. A support ticket is created to track the migration progress; use the returned ticket ID with /tickets/{id} to monitor status.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The website service ID. Use website_id from GET /websites. default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

Request body

postWebsiteMigration_request postWebsiteMigration_request (required)
Body Parameter —

Return type

postWebsiteMigration_200_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "ticket" : 0,
  "text" : "text"
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Migration request submitted. postWebsiteMigration_200_response

401

Unauthorized getAccountInfo_401_response
Up 
post /websites/{id}/reverse_dns
Update Website Reverse DNS (postWebsitesReverseDns)
Updates the reverse DNS entries for each of the IP addresses for the website.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The website service ID. Use website_id from GET /websites. default: null

Consumes

This API call consumes the following media types via the Content-Type request header:
  • application/json
  • multipart/form-data

Request body

ReverseDnsEntries ReverseDnsEntries (required)
Body Parameter —

TextResponse

}

Up 
delete /websites/{id}
Cancel Website (webhostingCancel)
Cancels a webhosting service. The service will be scheduled for termination and all hosted content will be removed. This action cannot be undone.

Path parameters

id (required)
Path Parameter — The website service ID. Use website_id from GET /websites. default: null

Return type

webhostingCancel_200_response

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "success" : true,
  "text" : "Website is canceled."
}

Example data

Content-Type: application/json
{
  "code" : 0,
  "message" : "message"
}

Produces

This API call produces the following media types according to the Accept request header; the media type will be conveyed by the Content-Type response header.
  • application/json

Responses

200

Website cancel webhostingCancel_200_response

name
String Your name.
address
String Your address.
city
String Your city.
state
String Your state.
zip
String Your ZIP code.

String Your country.
phone
String Your phone number.
company (optional)
String Your company name.

String Additional address information.
locale (optional)
String Your preferred locale.
email_invoices (optional)
String Your email for invoice notifications.
email_abuse (optional)
String Your email for abuse notifications.
disable_reset (optional)
Boolean Set to true to disable account resets, or false to enable them.
disable_reinstall (optional)
Boolean Set to true to disable server reinstalls, or false to enable them.
disable_server_notifications (optional)
Boolean Set to true to disable server notifications, or false to enable them.
disable_email_notifications (optional)
Boolean Set to true to disable email notifications, or false to enable them.
gstin (optional)
String Your GST identification number (if applicable).

Request payload for creating a new prepay balance.

String Module the prepay should be applied to (for example default).

String Whether the prepay balance should be used automatically.

String First micro-charge amount for verification.
cc_amount2 (optional)
String Second micro-charge amount for verification.
terms (optional)
Boolean Whether terms were accepted for verification.

Integer

DenyRuleNew - DenyRuleNew Up

The data for a email deny rule record.
type
String The type of deny rule.
Enum:
domain
email
startswith
destination
data
String The content of the rule. If a domain type rule then an example would be google.com. For a begins with type an example would be msgid-. For the email typer an example would be user@server.com.
user (optional)
String Mail account username that will be tied to this rule. If not specified the first active mail order will be used.

DenyRuleRecord - DenyRuleRecord Up

The data for a email deny rule record.
type
String The type of deny rule.
Enum:
domain
email
startswith
destination
data
String The content of the rule. If a domain type rule then an example would be google.com. For a begins with type an example would be msgid-. For the email typer an example would be user@server.com.
id
Integer The deny rule Id number.
created
String the date the rule was created.
user (optional)
String Mail account username that will be tied to this rule. If not specified the first active mail order will be used.

Billing information for a domain service including payment status, dates, and cost.

String Date of the last invoice for the domain.

service_frequency (optional)

String Billing currency code.
service_currency_symbol (optional)
String Billing currency symbol.
service_cost_info (optional)

DomainContactDetails - Up

Contact details used for domain registrant/admin/technical/billing records.
status (optional)
String Contact status reported by the registrar.
state (optional)
String State or region for the contact address.
org_name (optional)
String Organization name for the contact.
country (optional)
String Two-letter country code for the contact.
postal_code (optional)
String Postal or ZIP code for the contact address.
email (optional)
String Email address for the contact.
fax (optional)
String Fax number for the contact, if available.
address2 (optional)
String Secondary street address line.
address3 (optional)
String Tertiary street address line.
address1 (optional)
String Primary street address line.
city (optional)
String City for the contact address.
phone (optional)
String Phone number for the contact.
first_name (optional)
String First name for the contact.
last_name (optional)
String Last name for the contact.

DomainDnssecRequest - Up

Request payload for adding DNSSEC DS records to a domain.
algorithm (optional)
array[Integer] List of DNSSEC algorithm IDs for each record.
digest_type (optional)
array[Integer] List of digest type IDs for each record.
digest (optional)
array[String] List of hex digests for each record.
key_tag (optional)
array[Integer] List of key tag values corresponding to each record.

DomainLookupResponse - Up

Availability, pricing, and order-field metadata for a domain lookup.
available (optional)
Boolean Whether the domain is available to register.
premium (optional)
Boolean Whether the domain is marked as premium by the registrar.
website (optional)
Boolean Indicates if the domain is already used by a website service.
domain_service (optional)
Boolean Indicates if the domain already exists as a domain service on the account.
service (optional)
Object Service catalog details for the domain's TLD.
whois_privacy (optional)
Boolean Whether Whois privacy is available for the TLD.
new (optional)
String Calculated registration price, when available.
renewal (optional)
String Calculated renewal price, when available.
transfer (optional)
String Calculated transfer price, when available.
fields (optional)
Object Registrar field requirements for this domain/TLD.
currencies (optional)
Object Pricing information normalized to supported currencies.

value (optional)

MailLog - MailLog Up

Paginated mail log response. Contains the full matched count (total) plus a page of MailLogEntry records. The total reflects the grouping mode: with groupby=recipient it counts delivery attempts, with groupby=message it counts unique messages.
total
Integer Total number of log entries that match the supplied filters, regardless of skip and limit. Reflects the groupby mode.
skip
Integer The skip value used for this page (echoed from the request).
limit
Integer The limit value used for this page (echoed from the request).
emails
array[MailLogEntry]

MailLogEntry - MailLogEntry Up

A single email record in the mail log. Combines data from the message store (envelope metadata), the queue release table (delivery status and response), and the sender delivery table (MX routing details). When groupby=recipient each row represents one delivery attempt; when groupby=message delivery fields reflect one arbitrary recipient.
_id
Integer Internal auto-increment database row ID.
id
String The relay-assigned mail ID (18-19 hex characters). Matches the mailid filter parameter and the text value returned by send endpoints.
from
String SMTP envelope MAIL FROM address.
to
String SMTP envelope RCPT TO address.
created
String Human-readable creation timestamp in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format.
time
Integer Unix timestamp of message acceptance. Corresponds to the startDate and endDate filter parameters.
user
String The SMTP AUTH username used to submit the message (e.g. mb5658).
transtype
String SMTP transaction type negotiated with the relay.
origin
String IP address of the client that submitted the message to the relay.
interface
String Relay interface name that accepted the message.
subject (optional)
String The Subject header value. MIME-encoded subjects (UTF-8, ISO-8859, US-ASCII) are automatically decoded.
messageId (optional)
String The Message-ID header value. Can be used with the messageId filter for subsequent lookups.
sendingZone (optional)
String The sending zone assigned by the relay for outbound delivery.
bodySize (optional)
Integer Size of the message body in bytes.
seq (optional)
Integer Sequence index of this recipient in a multi-recipient message. Starts at 1.
delivered (optional)
Integer Delivery status flag. 1 = successfully delivered to destination MX. 0 = queued, deferred, or failed. null = delivery not yet attempted.
code (optional)
Integer The SMTP response code from the destination MX server (e.g. 250).
recipient (optional)
String The specific recipient address this delivery record is for.
response (optional)
String The full SMTP response string received from the destination MX server.
domain (optional)
String The destination domain for this delivery attempt.
locked (optional)
Integer Whether the queue entry is currently locked for delivery processing.
lockTime (optional)
String Millisecond-precision timestamp of the last queue lock acquisition.
assigned (optional)
String The relay server node assigned to deliver this message.
queued (optional)
String ISO 8601 timestamp when the message was placed into the delivery queue.
mxHostname (optional)
String The MX hostname the relay connected to for delivery. Corresponds to the mx filter parameter.

SendMail - SendMail Up

Details for an Email
to
String The Contact whom is the primary recipient of this email.
from
String The contact whom is the this email is from.
subject
String The subject or title of the email
body
String The main email contents.

SendMailAdv - SendMailAdv Up

Details for an Email
subject
String The subject or title of the email
body
String The main email contents.
from
EmailAddressName
to
array[EmailAddressName] A list of destionation email addresses to send this to
replyto (optional)
array[EmailAddressName] (optional) A list of email addresses that specify where replies to the email should be sent instead of the from address.
cc (optional)
array[EmailAddressName] (optional) A list of email addresses to carbon copy this message to. They are listed on the email and anyone getting the email can see this full list of Contacts who received the email as well.
bcc (optional)
array[EmailAddressName] (optional) list of email addresses that should receive copies of the email. They are hidden on the email and anyone gettitng the email would not see the other people getting the email in this list.
attachments (optional)
array[MailAttachment] (optional) File attachments to include in the email. The file contents must be base64 encoded!
id (optional)
Long (optional) ID of the Mail order within our system to use as the Mail Account. format: int64

Integer Monthly cost in cents for the control panel license.

VpsServiceAddons - Up

Add-on services and IP address information for a VPS, including control panel licenses and extra IP assignments.
has_cpanel (optional)
Boolean Whether a cPanel license is active on this VPS.
has_directadmin (optional)
Boolean Whether a DirectAdmin license is active on this VPS.
has_fantastico (optional)
Boolean Whether a Fantastico license is active on this VPS.
has_softaculous (optional)
Boolean Whether a Softaculous license is active on this VPS.
has_hdspace (optional)
Boolean Whether extra disk space has been added to this VPS.
dedicated_ip (optional)
Boolean Whether a dedicated IP address is assigned to this VPS.
extra_ips (optional)
array[String] List of additional IPv4 addresses assigned to this VPS.
extra_ips6 (optional)
array[String] List of additional IPv6 addresses assigned to this VPS.
unpaid_ips (optional)
array[String] List of IP addresses that have unpaid charges.
ips (optional)
array[String] All IPv4 addresses assigned to this VPS.
ips6 (optional)
array[String] All IPv6 addresses assigned to this VPS.
cpanel_id (optional)
Integer The add-on service ID for the cPanel license.
cost (optional)
Integer Total monthly add-on cost in cents.
ids (optional)
array[String] List of add-on service IDs active on this VPS.
rdata (optional)
array[String] Raw add-on data entries.

initiatePayment_200_response - Up

type (optional)
String The response type indicating how to handle the payment. Possible values: redirect (redirect user to a URL), submit (submit a form to a URL), single (immediate result).
Enum:
redirect
submit
single
redirect (optional)
String URL to redirect the user to for payment (when type is redirect).
action (optional)
String Form action URL (when type is submit).
method (optional)
String HTTP method for the form submission (when type is submit).
items (optional)
Object Form field name-value pairs to submit (when type is submit).
text (optional)
String Status or result text.

String The fully-qualified hostname to assign to the server.
server_root_password (optional)
String The root or administrator password to set on the server.

postWebsiteMigration_200_response - Up

text (optional)
String Confirmation message.

Integer The support ticket ID created for tracking the migration. Use this with /tickets/{id} to check migration progress.

postWebsiteMigration_request - Up

custPortal (optional)
String URL of the customer's current hosting portal.
regEmail (optional)
String Registered email address at the current host.
password (optional)
String Password for the current hosting account.
ctrlPanel (optional)
String URL of the current control panel.
ftpUsername (optional)
String FTP username at the current host.
ftpPassword (optional)
String FTP password at the current host.
siteBusyMig (optional)
String Information about site traffic during migration.
splReqMig (optional)
String Special requirements for the migration.
domainReg (optional)
String Whether domain registration assistance is needed.
dataMig (optional)
String Data migration timing preference.
domainRegPortal (optional)
String Domain registrar portal URL.
domainRegEmail (optional)
String Email for the domain registrar account.
domainRegPassword (optional)
String Password for the domain registrar account.
